author: slava77
body: Current HLT baseline tracking runs with the CA track algorithm extended to include OT hits; however these tracks can not be consumed as is by the LST algorithm; and the OT hits are dropped with `removeOTRechits` when the "pixel" seeds are made. This PR updates the LST algorithm to appropriately handle CA-extended tracks as the pixel seed/track input. A preliminary implementation was presented in the [June 9 TRK POG](https://indico.cern.ch/event/1690107/#94-status-update-on-lst-and-ls); since then the pending issue with the fake rate increase coming from short tracks was resolved. Summary of changes: - [major] PixelTriplet.h : the primary functional changes in the matching logic that combines "pLS" (inner/pixel track as a line segment) with the LST OT line segments; this should now handle "pLS" with the last point anywhere in the tracker [including the not yet running CA endcap extension from #49346 and my guess also for the "stubCA"] - [minor] Quintuplet.h: a bit more flexible `runDeltaBetaIterations` - [major] LSTPrepareInput.h (and related in LSTInputSoA.h, Common.h, Segment.h ): add hit type details and change the hit storage to a more flexible format (saving the first and the number of hits) to accommodate for the longer tracks and distinguish IT and OT hits. - [minor] TrackCandidate.h: more optimal hit overlap checking between pLSs `checkPixelHits` method replaced by `pixelHitsOverlapAny` - [major] (change added after the POG presentation) LSTOutputConverter.cc (also hltInitialStepTrajectorySeedsLST_cfi.py and related): break up CA-extented tracks with 3 IT hits and some OT hits that were not matched by anything in the OT with the LST algorithm; go back to just the IT hits. These were found to be predominantly fake. This change gives these tracks/hits in IT another chance to be reconstructed. - [major] hltInitialStepSeeds_cfi.py (and related, above): switch off `removeOTRechits` during the seed creation. Expected runtime changes are only in the HLT workflows (almost no change in the offline workflows with LST): - (not in default monitored collections) large decrease in the duplicate rate in the LST output - about the same efficiency and fake rate in the final tracks; small increase in the duplicate rate - timing: similar or minor speedup in the mkFit modules from fewwer duplicates in the input
title: LST updates to handle "pixel tracks" with OT hits
